Off Calendar Feb-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jordan Rosenberg, 415-658-5194 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Amanda Hamilton, Esq. 415-543-8700 of Reed Smithfor Defendants BENJAMIN TALCOTT, ROBERT LIBBY, RUTH GUADAGNO.(appeared late after the case was called)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views it did not receive an email contesting the tentative and asks why the late filed Opposition papers should be considered. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS BENJAMIN TALCOTT, ROBERT LIBBY, RUTH GUADAGNO'S Motion To Set Aside Entry of Default.

Taken Under Submission.***Defendant's Counsel appeared after the case was called and submitted 2 proposed orders to the Court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

***After the hearing, The Court signs an Order granting specially appearing Defendants' motion to set aside entry of default. (see scanned order for details)***

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jordan Rosenberg, 415-658-5194 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Amanda Hamilton, Esq. 415-543-8700 of Reed Smithfor Defendants BENJAMIN TALCOTT, ROBERT LIBBY, RUTH GUADAGNO.(appeared late after the case was called)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views it did not receive an email contesting the tentative and asks why the late filed Opposition papers should be considered. Plaintiff argues. SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANTS BENJAMIN TALCOTT, ROBERT LIBBY, RUTH GUADAGNO's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONSES FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION.

Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JORDAN ROSENBERG, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. LINCOLN BANDLOW OF FOX ROTHSCHILD LLP, TEL. NO. 1-310-598-4150 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; SPECIALLY-APPEARING DEFENDANT HUDSON CITY SAVINGS BANK'S Motion To Quash Summons And Dismiss First Amended Complaint is Granted. Plaintiff fails to show that Defendant Hudson City Savings Bank had any contacts with California to warrant the exercise of general or specific jurisdiction over it. Order signed in open court.
